findings.message: New user-facing Dashboard view ships with no RUM/error instrumentation reachable from it. The fetch to /internal/summary has no .catch and no per-view error signal: a non-2xx response (e.g. 500) does not reject the promise, so r.json() resolves and the failure renders silently with no RUM signal. The user-visible behavior of this new view is unobserved.
findings.suggestion: Check res.ok before parsing and emit an explicit RUM error/event on failure (e.g. push a {kind:'error'} event or increment the js-errors counter via the RUM beacon), and surface a visible error state instead of rendering JSON.stringify(null).

findings.message: The fetch promise chain (.then(r => r.json()).then(setData)) has no error handling. While a hard network rejection would surface via the global unhandledrejection listener, a failed HTTP status (4xx/5xx) is not caught at all and produces no RUM error signal, so this new code path's failures are invisible to operability monitoring.
findings.suggestion: Add a .catch that records a RUM error event and sets a visible error state, and reject on !res.ok so HTTP failures are observed.
